questions.question: Explain the working principle of a Cathode Ray Tube (CRT) display with a diagram.
keyPoints: CRT uses electron gun to emit electrons
Electron beam is deflected by magnetic coils
Beam strikes phosphorescent screen causing emission of light
Scanning pattern: horizontal and vertical deflection
Raster scan mechanism for image formation

questions.question: Define the following: (i) Resolution (ii) Refresh Rate (iii) Color Depth (iv) Aspect Ratio
keyPoints: Resolution: Total pixels (horizontal × vertical)
Refresh Rate: Screen updates per second in Hz
Color Depth: Bits per pixel determining colors
Aspect Ratio: Width to height ratio of display

questions.question: Explain the concept of Double Buffering in graphics systems.
keyPoints: Uses two frame buffers instead of one
Front buffer: currently displayed image
Back buffer: being drawn/updated
Swapping occurs after drawing completes
Eliminates visual flicker and tearing artifacts

questions.question: List and explain four types of modern display technologies.
keyPoints: LCD: Liquid crystal with backlight
LED: Light emitting diodes for backlight
OLED: Organic LEDs with per-pixel emission
Each has different contrast, response time, power consumption

questions.question: What is Z-Buffering? Explain its role in hidden surface removal.
keyPoints: Z-buffer stores depth values for each pixel
Compares depth of new primitive with existing pixel
If closer, replaces depth and color value
Ensures correct visibility of 3D objects
Also called depth buffering

questions.question: Describe the different types of Video RAM (VRAM) used in graphics systems.
keyPoints: SDRAM: Synchronous DRAM for early graphics
DDR: Double Data Rate for better bandwidth
GDDR: Graphics-optimized DDR variants
HBM: High Bandwidth Memory with stacked dies

questions.question: Explain the basic graphics pipeline with a neat flowchart.
keyPoints: Application stage: Geometry data submission
Geometry processing: Vertex transformation
Rasterization: Convert to pixels
Pixel processing: Coloring and effects
Frame buffer: Final image storage

questions.question: With a neat block diagram, explain the architecture of a typical computer graphics system.
keyPoints: Block diagram showing: CPU, GPU, Frame Buffer, Display Controller, Monitor
CPU sends graphics commands
GPU processes and renders graphics
Frame buffer stores pixel data
Display controller handles timing
Monitor displays final output
Role of VRAM in graphics pipeline

questions.question: Explain the difference between Raster Scan and Vector Scan displays. Also discuss the advantages and limitations of each.
keyPoints: Raster scan: Pixel grid scanning, row by row
Vector scan: Direct beam deflection to draw lines
Raster advantages: Color capability, realistic images
Raster limitations: Fixed resolution, aliasing
Vector advantages: Smooth lines, infinite resolution
Vector limitations: Limited colors, flicker at complex images

questions.question: Discuss the various stages of the Graphics Pipeline in detail with a diagram.
keyPoints: Application stage: Scene description input
Vertex processing: Transformation and lighting
Primitive assembly: Form triangles/lines
Rasterization: Fragment generation
Fragment processing: Texturing and shading
Per-sample operations: Z-test, blending
Frame buffer write: Final pixel output
Diagram showing data flow between stages

questions.question: Explain the working of a GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) and its role in modern graphics systems.
keyPoints: GPU is specialized parallel processor
Contains multiple shader cores (vertex, fragment)
Parallel processing thousands of pixels simultaneously
Graphics pipeline acceleration
Memory hierarchy: Shared memory, caches, VRAM
Compute capabilities beyond graphics (GPGPU)
Comparison with CPU: More cores, lower clock speed
